title: Por vs Para — the classic distinction
objectives: Choose between por and para correctly in common contexts
Memorize the core uses of each

vocab.en: because of, through, in exchange for, by

grammar_notes: PARA = forward-looking: purpose, recipient, destination, deadline. 'Este regalo es para ti.' 'Estudio para aprobar.' 'Salgo para Madrid.'
POR = backward-looking or in-the-middle: cause, exchange, movement through, agent. 'Lo hago por amor.' 'Diez euros por el libro.' 'Caminé por el parque.' 'Hecho por un artista.'
Quick test: if you can replace with 'because of' or 'through' → 'por'. If you can replace with 'in order to' or 'for the purpose of' → 'para'.
Russian doesn't make this distinction with prepositions — relies on context. Hardest item in A2 for Russian speakers.
example_phrases: Estudio español para hablar con mi novio. (purpose)
Compré el libro por diez euros. (exchange)
